notes:
I installed it and tried launching it without any extra arguments or changing proton versions. After I press play it says launching, and as soon as it's "running" it closes and the play button turns green again.

I also tried these proton versions:

Experimental
Hotfix
GE-Proton10-6

And these launch arguments (separately):

%command% /WineDetectionEnabled:False

PROTON_ENABLE_WAYLAND=1 PROTON_USE_NTSYNC=1 %command% /WineDetectionEnabled:False

All had the same result. The game just wouldn't open.

notes: Game attempts to open, freezes all activity on my monitors, then becomes a black bar on the top left of the screen. Tried loading different versions of proton, updated GE, updating Steam itself, verifying files, and trying some of the commands in the replies: nothing. Hell, once, it froze everything as usual, and my gpu started spitting out the pink and green crashing effects we love to see. I admit I am new to Linux period, and do not know how to do much tinkering beyond this, but I read that people are running this game fine OOB and am baffled as to how.  Will play on my Deck for now.

title: PRAGMATA

notes: The game started up ok. I had to wait some minutes for the shaders to compile, then was asked for my age and to configure brightness. After that it begins loading again and then freezes. The CPU load stays at one core fully utilized, but the screen freezes and after alt+tabbing, it even becomes black, i.e., no update seems to be called. Had to pkill it. I tried other Proton versions and some of the tinker steps here, but nothing worked. Note that the game only starts with proton 9.0-4. All other versions, including experimental, crash the game during startup before anything is shown.
